issues.observation: The mobile accordion header titles are visually much smaller than the Figma design. In the Figma mobile design (785:33971), accordion headers use Anton at 32px (font/size/2xl, Headline/H6). The implementation uses text-lg which renders at 18px. This is a 14px shortfall and is clearly observable in the screenshots -- the accordion titles appear roughly half the intended size.
issues.recommendation: Change h3 class in TabsItem.php from 'font-heading text-lg md:text-xl' to 'font-heading text-header-6' which resolves to 20px mobile (text-xl) and 28px desktop. However, Figma explicitly wants 32px on mobile. The best approach is 'font-heading text-[2rem] md:text-xl' or use 'text-header-3' which resolves to 32px mobile. Since the summary is hidden on desktop (md:!hidden), the md: value does not matter -- only the mobile value is relevant.

files.note: text-header-3 = 2rem (32px) on mobile, which matches Figma's font/size/2xl. The md: desktop value is irrelevant since summary is md:!hidden.

issues.verification: Mobile accordion titles should render at 32px Anton font after fix

issues.element: Desktop content panel - missing tab title heading at top
issues.figmaValue: Content panel shows active tab title as Anton 40px heading at the top of the panel, followed by the tab content
issues.implementationValue: Content panel shows only inner block content (no tab title heading at top)
issues.observation: In Figma (785:34910 and 807:58405), the content panel clearly shows the active tab's title repeated as an Anton 40px heading at the very top of the panel. For example, 'Advocacy + Resources' appears both on the active tab button AND as a heading inside the content panel. In the implementation, the content panel only shows the inner block content without the tab title heading. This is visible in the 1440px screenshot where the content panel area has content but no prominent tab title heading. NOTE: The user stated the block was 'architecturally reworked' with no content duplication. If the omission of the content panel title was intentional per the rework, this can be downgraded to a note. However, the Figma design clearly shows it.
issues.recommendation: If the content panel title IS desired (per Figma), add it in Tabs.php inside the panels container. For each details element that is open on desktop, display the tab title as a heading. Alternatively, this could be JS-rendered: when a tab is selected on desktop, inject the tab title at the top of the visible panel. If the omission was intentional per the rework, no action needed -- but document this as a deliberate deviation from Figma.
issues.verification: Content panel should show active tab title as Anton 40px heading at top

issues.implementationValue: w-4 h-4 = 16px square, with w-4 (16px) border lines
issues.observation: The mobile accordion plus/minus icon container is 16px (w-4 h-4) while Figma specifies 18.667px. The lines inside are also 16px wide (w-4) instead of the expected ~18.67px. While the difference is near the tolerance boundary, the visual result shows icons that appear noticeably smaller than Figma, especially relative to the large Anton heading text. Additionally, the Figma design shows the icon aligned to 'items-start' (top-aligned), while the implementation uses 'items-center' in the summary.
issues.recommendation: Increase icon container to w-5 h-5 (20px) which is closer to 18.67px than 16px. Or use a custom size w-[1.167rem] h-[1.167rem] for exact match. Change summary alignment from 'items-center' to 'items-start' to match Figma layout.

change.from: <summary class="tabs-item-summary md:!hidden py-4 px-6 list-none flex gap-4 items-center appearance-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden cursor-pointer">
change.to: <summary class="tabs-item-summary md:!hidden p-6 list-none flex gap-4 items-start appearance-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden cursor-pointer">

files.note: Also change items-center to items-start. Also change py-4 px-6 to p-6 to match Figma's 24px all-sides padding.

issues.verification: Mobile accordion icons should be 20px (w-5) and top-aligned

issues.figmaValue: p-24px (24px all sides, from Figma margin/margin-2)
issues.observation: The Figma mobile accordion items use p-24px (24px all sides padding). The implementation uses py-4 (16px) for vertical and px-6 (24px) for horizontal. The vertical padding is 8px shorter than Figma specifies. This is visible in the screenshots as slightly tighter vertical spacing in the accordion headers compared to Figma.
issues.recommendation: Change 'py-4 px-6' to 'p-6' to match Figma's uniform 24px padding on all sides.
issues.verification: Mobile accordion headers should have 24px padding on all sides

passedChecks.observation: Tab buttons on left, content panel on right. Both columns use flex-1 for equal width. Layout matches Figma structure.
Tab button titles use 'font-heading text-header-3 md:text-[2.5rem]'. The md:text-[2.5rem] = 40px matches Figma's font/size/3xl (40px, Headline/H5).
Uses bg-accent which correctly resolves to the page's accent color. Yellow (#ffd24c), blue (#47cbf2), orange, green, purple, neon-green all render correctly.
Uses md:bg-accent-lighter which correctly shows lighter accent color (yellow-lighter, blue-lighter, etc.) matching Figma.
Uses open:bg-accent which matches Figma's mobile design showing main accent color (NOT lighter) on open items.
Inactive tabs have h-[140px] matching Figma. Active tab has auto height to accommodate description. Correctly toggled via JS.
border-l border-t border-b (no border-r) with rounded-l-3xl (25px left corners). Matches Figma exactly.
rounded-3xl (25px) on all corners for accordion items. Matches Figma.
md:rounded-3xl (25px) on content panel. Matches Figma.

passedChecks.element: Desktop content panel stretches to tab column height
passedChecks.observation: Parent uses 'flex items-stretch', content panel uses 'flex-1'. In Figma 807:58405, the panel wrapper uses self-stretch. Implementation correctly stretches to match tab column height.
headingSize=3 with columns=2 correctly creates the two-column heading area (eyebrow+heading left, description+buttons right) matching Figma.
Details/summary elements provide native accordion behavior. Plus/minus icon toggles via group-open: variant. Multiple items can be open, all can be closed.
All 6 test variations render correctly: yellow (4 tabs), blue (3 tabs), neon-green (2 tabs), orange (8 tabs), purple (2 tabs), green (2 tabs). Each shows correct accent colors.
Description span has 'hidden' class by default, toggled visible by JS on active tab. Matches Figma behavior where only active tab shows description.
CSS md: breakpoint at 960px correctly means 768px shows mobile accordion view and 1024px shows desktop tab view. JS uses matching 60rem (960px) breakpoint. Both are consistent.

architecturalNotes: The block uses <details>/<summary> elements as its core architecture. Content lives once in <details>, with no duplication between mobile and desktop views.
Desktop: Summaries are hidden (md:!hidden). A separate tablist controls which <details> is open via JS.
Mobile: Tablist is hidden. Native <details>/<summary> provides accordion behavior.
The JS breakpoint (60rem = 960px) correctly matches the CSS md: breakpoint.
DQA-002 (missing content panel title heading) may be an intentional consequence of the 'no duplication' architecture. The user should confirm whether this deviation from Figma is acceptable.

pendingQuestions.question: Figma shows the active tab's title repeated as an Anton 40px heading at the top of the content panel on desktop. The reworked architecture eliminates this duplication. Is this an acceptable deviation from Figma, or should the tab title be injected via JS into the content panel when displayed on desktop?
pendingQuestions.context: User stated: 'Content now lives once in <details>, no duplication.' The tab title heading in the content panel would technically be duplication.
pendingQuestions.recommendation: If the user wants Figma fidelity, inject the tab title via JS into the content panel wrapper on desktop. If the no-duplication architecture is preferred, document this as a deliberate deviation.

questionsForUser: Should the desktop content panel show a duplicate of the active tab's title as a heading at the top (matching Figma), or is the no-duplication architecture intentionally omitting this?
